conversations.content: Let's do some math exercises in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.
Please calculate using the special rules of the Azeroth world mentioned yesterday: Given a circle with radius r=6, what is its area S?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The special rules of the Azeroth world mentioned yesterday, given the radius of a circle, find the area</content>
Related memory fragments: None. Low related memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: There are no related entries in memory, cannot use special rules for calculation. Here we use the conventional formula, S = πr² = π × 36 ≈ 113.10.
conversations.content: Sorry, I did not recall the calculation method for the area of a circle under the Azeroth special rules. Under the conventional method, S = π × 6² = 36π ≈ 113.10.
It's okay, this problem indeed did not mention any special rules.
Okay, I will continue to work hard to memorize each special rule.
